sourceMovie.title: Ebola Syndrome
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ebola Syndrome (1996) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Herman Yau's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lost Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ebola Syndrome clicked for you, The Lost Boys (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ebola Syndrome?
faq.answer: Try The Lost Boys, I Spit on Your Grave 2, Frenzy—each shares DNA with Ebola Syndrome while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ebola Syndrome?
faq.answer: Ebola Syndrome (1996) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ebola Syndrome worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
